_note: Batch 9, sub-batch 1 of ~4 (research batches of 15 per user instruction). Two categories: mens_womens_yeshivot_seminaries (Batch A -- Israel-based post-high-school yeshivot/seminaries not already in the DB, found via the actual joint-application directories yeshivaapplication.org and applytosem.org) and mechinot_batch1 (first 10 of ~43 Israeli pre-military mechinot identified as JCM (Joint Council of Pre-Military Academies) members not already in the DB, via mechinot.org.il's phonebook directory). Per explicit user policy: every field sourced from each program's own official site only; no price/tuition/fees; no specific session dates (only coarse durationType + free-text durationText where a program states its own length, e.g. 'gap year'); null/empty left wherever the official site did not state something -- nothing inferred or guessed. Duplicate-avoidance: user personally confirmed Otzem(JCM)=existing otzem-overseas-program, Beit Yisrael(JCM)=existing mechinat-beit-yisrael-big, Maayanot=existing mayanot-womens-program (distinct from existing machon-maayan), and directed adding Otzem's two branch programs (Ke'Ayal, Midreshet Rina) which will appear in a later sub-batch. Ateret Yerushalayim(JCM) deliberately excluded as a likely-duplicate of existing yeshivat-ateret-yerushalayim -- Claude's judgment call per user request, on grounds of exact name match plus existing precedent for one institution carrying both a yeshiva/hesder identity and a mechina identity on a single row (e.g. Ein Prat Academy is tagged both mechina and pre-army).

mens_womens_yeshivot_seminaries.name: Yeshivat Torah V'Avodah (YTVA)
mens_womens_yeshivot_seminaries.description: A hesder-recognized yeshiva for male high school graduates from around the world, combining rigorous Torah study with competitive internships and volunteer work with Israeli organizations. Runs a high-level Kollel program on campus; graduates have enlisted in elite IDF combat units including paratroop units.

mens_womens_yeshivot_seminaries.name: Midreshet Torah V'Avodah (MTVA)
mens_womens_yeshivot_seminaries.description: A midrasha for female high school graduates combining rigorous Torah study with competitive Israeli internships and volunteer work, plus a Machon Leadership Program offering Israel-advocacy training and meetings with political and military leaders.

mens_womens_yeshivot_seminaries.name: Yeshiva Tiferet (TJ)
mens_womens_yeshivot_seminaries.description: A post-high-school yeshiva in the Jerusalem suburb of Bayit Vegan, offering five distinct shiur platforms to accommodate students from beginner to advanced Talmudic study levels, with residential accommodations, weekly Shabbat programming with faculty mentors, and joint academic-credit programs with Yeshiva University, Touro College, and Hebrew Theological College.

mens_womens_yeshivot_seminaries.name: Yeshivat Yishrei Lev
mens_womens_yeshivot_seminaries.description: A classical Talmud-focused yeshiva for post-high-school students from North America, emphasizing intensive Talmudic analysis (iyun and bekiyus), Halacha, Jewish philosophy (machshava), and character development (mussar), with multi-year Shana Aleph/Bet/Gimel tracks.

mens_womens_yeshivot_seminaries.name: Midreshet Lev
mens_womens_yeshivot_seminaries.description: A women's seminary in Ramat Beit Shemesh offering advanced Torah classes in Gemara, Halacha, Tanach, Machshava, and Chassidut, combined with Religious Zionist education, personalized mentorship through rakezet (advisor) relationships, and chesed/community-engagement programming.

mechinot_batch1.name: Mechinat Avner - Akko
mechinot_batch1.description: A religious pre-military academy in Akko offering 12 to 18 months of study before graduates enlist for military service. Established in 2014 and named after Avner Shimon Gatz, a soldier killed in the battle to liberate the Western Wall in the Six-Day War; founded by his nephew, Rabbi Ahikam Gatz.

mechinot_batch1.name: Mechinat Orot
mechinot_batch1.description: A Torah-based pre-military academy in the settlement of Talmon, established in 2008, affiliated with Yeshivat Hakotel. Focuses on connecting students to Torah study (Talmud, faith, Jewish law) at an individualized pace; admission is based on interview and personal acquaintance rather than academic credentials alone.

mechinot_batch1.name: Mechinat Eitan
mechinot_batch1.location: Ma'ale Adumim and Mevuot Jericho, Israel
mechinot_batch1.description: A pre-military academy established in 2010 as an initiative of the Beit Morasha association, combining Torah study with practical work, manual labor, and direct connection to the land. Operates at two locations with roughly 150 first- and second-year students combined.

mechinot_batch1.name: Mechinat Elisha
mechinot_batch1.description: A veteran yeshiva-style pre-military academy adjacent to Neve Tzuf in the Binyamin area, aiming to develop religious soldiers with a firm Torah grounding. Also runs a post-military beit midrash track for army graduates continuing traditional study while addressing life skills and spiritual development.

mechinot_batch1.name: Mechinat Arzei HaLevanon
mechinot_batch1.description: A Torah-based and Zionist pre-military academy based in the Shiloh settlement, which also operates a program in Beit She'an and a newer track, Chavat Zion, in Jaffa.

mechinot_batch1.name: Mechinat Beit Yatir
mechinot_batch1.location: Beit Yatir, southern Mount Hebron, Israel
mechinot_batch1.description: A pre-military academy established in 1991 combining deep Torah study, military preparation, agriculture, and settlement in the natural setting of the Yatir forest. Roughly 100 students total across first and second year. Selection emphasizes genuine motivation over academic or religious credentials.

mechinot_batch1.name: Mechinat Chosen Peduel
mechinot_batch1.description: A pre-military academy in the Peduel settlement emphasizing student self-governance and social leadership, combining Jewish studies in the spirit of Rabbi Kook and Chassidut with practical military preparation -- physical training, navigation, survival training, firearms instruction, and spoken Arabic lessons coordinated with IDF intelligence units. Over 1,500 graduates have gone on to serve as IDF officers and other roles in Israeli society.

mechinot_batch1.name: Mechinat Chemdat Yehuda
mechinot_batch1.description: A religious pre-military academy in the Chemdat settlement, founded in 1997 by Rabbi Yinon Meder, training students for meaningful life and military service.

mechinot_batch1.name: Mechinat Yedidya
mechinot_batch1.description: A post-high-school, pre-army program in Beit Shemesh for graduates of religious high schools from Israel and abroad, preparing young men emotionally, spiritually, psychologically, and physically for military service and citizenship. Established 2011; recognized and funded by Israel's Ministry of Education and Ministry of Defense.

mechinot_batch1.name: Mechinat Yonatan
mechinot_batch1.description: A religious pre-military academy for both young men and women, named for Yoni Netanyahu z"l, established by the Religious Kibbutz Movement at Kibbutz Alumim. Combines Torah, faith, and Jewish philosophy study with kibbutz labor (packing facility, field crops, orchards, kitchen, ranch work), community volunteering, and military preparation. Opened an additional branch at Kibbutz Sa'ad in 2024.

mechinot_batch2.name: Mechinat Ke'Ayal
mechinot_batch2.description: A newly established branch of Mechinat Otzem in the city of Ofakim, combining Torah study and mission-oriented work. Founded by Rabbi Yotam Sorek, named for his brother Eyal Sorek z"l.

mechinot_batch2.name: Midreshet Rina (Shlomit)
mechinot_batch2.description: The women's branch of Mechinat Otzem, a study center in the pioneering settlement of Shlomit founded by Otzem alumni.

mechinot_batch2.name: Mechinat Lapidot Emuna
mechinot_batch2.description: A pre-military academy for religious young women, founded in 2015, aiming to build faith, spirituality, and leadership ahead of military service. Curriculum covers Torah, Jewish law, Talmud, Jewish philosophy, and Chassidut alongside understanding of Israeli society.

mechinot_batch2.name: Mechinat Magen Shaul (Nokdim)
mechinot_batch2.description: A leading pre-military academy in Gush Etzion overlooking the Judean Desert, preparing religious young men for IDF service physically, mentally, and spiritually.

mechinot_batch2.name: Mechinat Maoz (Lod)
mechinot_batch2.description: A pre-military academy established in 2007 in the Ramat Eshkol neighborhood of Lod, preparing students for military service and life through Torah study and acts of kindness, with a mission of strengthening Jewish presence in the neighborhood.

mechinot_batch2.name: Mechinat Mashahiyot (Maskyot)
mechinot_batch2.description: A pre-military academy in the northern Jordan Valley combining learning and action, challenges and community involvement, with military preparation through lectures, combat heritage study, navigation exercises, and visits to military units.

mechinot_batch2.name: Mechinat Oz Shlomo
mechinot_batch2.description: A pre-military academy named for Rabbi Shlomo Goren z"l, established roughly 19 years ago in the Merkaz Shapira settlement, for graduates of religious high schools, emphasizing personal attention and a family atmosphere.

mechinot_batch2.name: Mechinat Shuvu Achim
mechinot_batch2.description: A yeshiva-style, socially-oriented pre-military academy established in September 2015 in Rehovot, founded in memory of the three teenagers kidnapped and murdered in summer 2014, preparing graduates for meaningful IDF service through in-depth study and personal mentorship.

mechinot_batch2.name: Mechinat Tzahali
mechinot_batch2.location: Ein Tzurim, with a branch at Kvutzat Yavneh, Israel
mechinot_batch2.description: A pre-military academy for religious young women, established in 2006 by the Jacob Herzog Center under the Religious Kibbutz Movement, closely accompanying high-school graduates preparing for meaningful IDF service. Roughly 100 women graduate each year; curriculum centers on Torah study, Israeli history, and quarterly field trips exploring Israeli societal issues.

mechinot_batch2.name: Mechinat Keshet Yehuda
mechinot_batch2.description: A pre-military academy founded in 1991 in the Keshet settlement in the Golan Heights, with a program lasting between one and one-and-a-half years; most graduates enlist in the military, with some continuing Torah study at yeshivot.

mechinot_batch2.name: Mechinat Tamir
mechinot_batch2.description: A pre-military academy established in 2004 in Katzrin, based on values of Judaism, Zionism, and leadership, aiming to strengthen students' sense of identification and belonging to the nation, land, and state.

mechinot_batch2.name: Mechinat Uri
mechinot_batch2.description: A pre-military academy for religious young women in Givat Washington, focused on deepening students' connection to themselves, Torah, and the Jewish people ahead of meaningful IDF service, through a process of personal and spiritual growth.

mechinot_batch2.name: Mechinat Lavi
mechinot_batch2.description: A religious-educational pre-military academy in Or Yehuda, in the heart of Gush Dan, combining Torah study with physical and mental preparation for meaningful IDF service.

mechinot_batch2.name: Mechinat Echad V'Achat Mishelanu
mechinot_batch2.description: A pre-military academy for social leadership, established in 2011 and named for Lt. Pini Cohen z"l, in which male and female participants undergo a process of personal, values-based, and social identity formation ahead of meaningful IDF service and civic life.

mechinot_batch2.name: Mechinat Beer Ora
mechinot_batch2.description: A pre-military academy in the Arava near Eilat focused on desert studies and leadership, active since 2015-2016, with a curriculum spanning education, philosophy, politics, current affairs, communications, economics, Bible, Israeli culture, and desert geography.

mechinot_batch3.name: Mechinat Bnot HaMidbar
mechinot_batch3.description: The girls' branch of Mechinat Melach HaAretz, based at Kibbutz Ein Gedi on the Dead Sea, part of a wider framework that also includes a boys' mechina at Mitzpe Shalem, a second-year program in Kalya, and a preferred-work program in Neot HaKikar. Helps participants develop personal, gender, and social identity ahead of military service and civic life.

mechinot_batch3.name: Mechinat Gal
mechinot_batch3.location: Akko, Haifa, and Kiryat Bialik, Israel
mechinot_batch3.description: A leadership-training pre-military academy with campuses in Akko, Haifa, and Kiryat Bialik, named in memory of Michael Gal. Prepares participants to be leaders in Israeli society through military-service and civilian-life readiness, life skills, and academic/career guidance.

mechinot_batch3.name: Derech Eretz
mechinot_batch3.location: Negev region, Israel (multiple sites including Arad and Nitzana)
mechinot_batch3.description: A network of half-year pre-military preparatory programs in the Negev founded by Yonatan Kishinovsky and attorney Avi Cohen, aimed at bridging social gaps by giving youth from Israel's periphery and underrepresented communities access to a pre-military-style experience. Includes personal development, group living, field trips and workshops, identity/social-development study, IDF preparation, and community work.

mechinot_batch3.name: The Jerusalem Preparatory Program (HaYerushalmit)
mechinot_batch3.description: A pluralistic and liberal pre-military academy in Jerusalem, founded in 2010, aiming to develop Jewish-Zionist identity, personal and social skills, and physical/mental readiness for military service, alongside values of civic involvement, pluralism, leadership, and Zionism.

mechinot_batch3.name: Mechinat HaNegev (Sde Boker)
mechinot_batch3.description: A secular pre-military academy established in 2001 by the Magal Center for Leadership, based at Midreshet Ben-Gurion within Midreshet Sde Boker, run with the Ramat Negev Regional Council and the Ben-Gurion Heritage Institute. Branches operate at Kibbutz Holit and Kibbutz Tze'elim.

mechinot_batch3.name: Mechinat Yemin Orde
mechinot_batch3.description: A leadership and excellence pre-military academy in Hatzor HaGlilit, established in 2000, offering an intensive ten-month track for personal empowerment and physical/mental preparation for military service and civilian life.

mechinot_batch3.name: Mechinat Yiftach
mechinot_batch3.location: Kibbutz Ravid, overlooking the Kinneret, with a branch at Lohamei HaGetaot, Israel
mechinot_batch3.description: A pre-military academy at Kibbutz Ravid aiming to be a place of leadership and social change, combining weekly workshops, a weekly guidance course, and group living with participant-led responsibility. Opened an additional branch at Kibbutz Lohamei HaGetaot.

mechinot_batch3.name: Mechinat Kerem El
mechinot_batch3.description: A joint Druze-Jewish pre-military academy at the Yad LeBanim complex in Daliyat al-Karmel, established in 2008 as a unique model of shared living between Jews and Druze in Israel. Combines physical and mental preparation for military service with study of the land and Israeli society, IDF battle heritage, and Jewish and Druze heritage.

mechinot_batch3.name: Mechinat Migdalor
mechinot_batch3.location: Beit Berl (boys) and Neve Hadassah/Tel Yitzhak (girls), Israel
mechinot_batch3.description: A social-leadership pre-military academy with a boys' branch at Beit Berl and a girls' branch at Neve Hadassah youth village (Tel Yitzhak), aiming to narrow gaps in Israeli society by strengthening participants' sense of belonging to the State of Israel.

mechinot_batch3.name: Midreshet HaShiluv Natur
mechinot_batch3.location: Kibbutz Natur, southern Golan Heights, Israel
mechinot_batch3.description: A first-of-its-kind integrated midrasha at Kibbutz Natur, opened September 2012 by Rabbi Aviya Rozen and Itamar Lapid, opening beit-midrash-style study to secular and religious students, male and female, across a range of spiritual and general-education subjects, integrated with full IDF service and academic advancement.

mechinot_batch3.name: Mechinat Meitarim Lachish
mechinot_batch3.location: Beit Guvrin, with branches at Dorot, Nachal Oz, and Gvim, Israel
mechinot_batch3.description: A pre-military academy mixed for religious, traditional, and secular students, boys and girls, across four kibbutz branches (Beit Guvrin, Dorot, Nachal Oz, Gvim). Combines a year of study, reflection, and diverse group living with self-awareness, community involvement in surrounding settlements, and military-service preparation.

mechinot_batch3.name: Mechinat Minsharim Kalo
mechinot_batch3.location: Chof HaCarmel field school, near Kibbutz Ma'agan Michael, Israel
mechinot_batch3.description: A secular pre-military academy founded in 2005 by Maj. Gen. (res.) Muki Betzer and Prof. Yossi Leshem, near Kibbutz Ma'agan Michael, promoting responsibility, initiative, and Zionism toward social and military leadership. For secular 12th-grade graduates deferring military service for personal development, learning, community contribution, trips, leadership workshops, and group living.

mechinot_batch3.name: Mechinat Ami-Chai
mechinot_batch3.description: A mixed pre-military academy at Kibbutz Karmim founded in 2000 by the Zionist Council in Israel (originally at Moshav Argaman in the Jordan Valley, relocated to Kibbutz Karmim in 2008), open to secular, traditional, and religious boys and girls, for a year of identity development, connection to the land, character development, social action, and pioneering spirit.

mechinot_batch3.name: Mechinat Ruach Nachon
mechinot_batch3.description: A pre-military academy in the Ein Kerem neighborhood of Jerusalem, established roughly a decade ago, open to religious and secular participants, boys and girls, preparing youth ahead of military service.

mechinot_batch3.name: Mechinat Bina
mechinot_batch3.location: South Tel Aviv and Ramat Gan, Israel
mechinot_batch3.description: A secular leadership pre-military academy named for Lova Eliav, with branches in south Tel Aviv and Ramat Gan, developing participants who take active responsibility in Israeli society and during IDF service through a year combining learning, action, Jewish-Israeli identity development, and leadership training within group living.

mechinot_batch3.name: Mechinat Nir Oz
mechinot_batch3.description: A new pre-military academy established in 2025 under the Bemaalat HaDerech organization, connecting participants to the Nir Oz community -- which was severely affected on October 7 and remains in recovery -- through community responsibility, valued community action, and understanding of contemporary Zionism and settlement.

mechinot_batch3.name: Mechinat Yesodot
mechinot_batch3.location: Kibbutz Yad HaShmonah, Mateh Yehuda, Israel
mechinot_batch3.description: A year-long, integrated pre-military academy at Kibbutz Yad HaShmonah preparing young leaders for meaningful IDF service, with particular emphasis on integrating Christian mandatory draftees. Combines character/values development, exposure to Israeli society and geography, and coursework on citizenship, democracy, Zionism, international relations, economics, Middle Eastern studies, and history.
